name: Mid Plains
description_short: Mid Plains Credit Union is a small Kansas-based credit union established in 1964, serving over 300 members with $1.2M in assets from its single Glasco branch.
description_long:
Mid Plains Credit Union was established in 1964 and is headquartered in Glasco, Kansas. As a state-chartered credit union, it operates as a member-owned financial cooperative focused on serving the local Kansas community. The institution has maintained steady operations for nearly 60 years with a commitment to personalized service.

Mid Plains offers a comprehensive range of banking and lending products typical of small credit unions. Members can access savings accounts, share draft (checking) accounts, money market accounts, and certificates of deposit (CDs), with current CD membership at 43+ accounts. On the lending side, they provide auto loans (new and used vehicles), personal loans, and mortgage options. The credit union also supports online banking, mobile app access for both iOS and Android, and online bill pay services.

What distinguishes Mid Plains is its ultra-local focus and personalized member experience. With only 1 branch and over 2 dedicated employees, this is intentionally a small-scale operation. One member review specifically praised the credit union for "helping build business" and noted the "banking hours second to nobody," suggesting unusual accessibility. The institution positions itself as offering a home-like atmosphere for members seeking loans and banking services.

Mid Plains is best suited for existing Kansas residents already within their membership field of eligibility and those seeking relationship-based banking from a deeply local institution. However, prospective members should note the extremely limited physical infrastructure (1 branch, 0 ATMs), which may create accessibility challenges. The $1.2M asset base indicates this is a micro-credit union, meaning product offerings and competitive rates may be more limited than larger institutions. The currently closed lobby status suggests operational constraints worth investigating before joining. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established 1964 with nearly 60 years of operational history and stability
Personalized, relationship-based banking with members reporting exceptional service quality
Offers full suite of products: savings, checking, CDs, auto loans, personal loans, mortgages
Mobile app available for iOS and Android platforms
Online banking and bill pay services for digital-first members
Member-owned cooperative structure aligns institution success with member interests
5.0-star rating based on member reviews praising business support and accessibility
cons: Only 1 branch location in Glasco, Kansas limits physical accessibility for non-local members
Zero ATMs in network; no stated ATM partnerships or shared branching agreements
Extremely small asset base ($1.2M) may limit product competitiveness and loan approval flexibility
Currently closed lobby status raises questions about operational constraints and hours
Membership eligibility restricted to specific field of membership (Kansas residents only per state charter)
best_for: Kansas residents within Mid Plains' membership field of eligibility seeking personalized local banking
Small business owners and self-employed individuals valuing relationship-based lending support
Members prioritizing community banking values over broad product selection and physical convenience
Existing members with long-term loyalty to the institution seeking comprehensive account bundling
diagnosis: Mid Plains Credit Union is best for Kansas residents already within its membership eligibility who value personalized, relationship-based banking from a deeply local institution. The primary caveat is severe geographic and infrastructure limitations: with only one branch and zero ATMs, this credit union is impractical for members outside Glasco or those requiring physical card/cash access, and the currently closed lobby status suggests operational constraints that warrant direct contact before membership.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
